SALES FILED AN UNTIMELY ONE-PAGE "OPPOSITION" THAT DOES NOT STATE ANY INTELLIGIBLE GROUND FOR OPPOSING THE DEMURRER OR SHOW ANY REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT HE COULD AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO OVERCOME THE DEFECTS FROM WHICH IT SUFFERS. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-16-2019 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-19-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Sales, 415-286-8495 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Ryan Stevens, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court asks questions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF JAMES SALES 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Sustain without leave to amend. No timely opposition filed. On June 7, 2019, Mr. Sales filed an untimely one-page "Opposition" that does not state any intelligible ground for opposing the demurrer or show any reasonable possibility that he could amend the complaint to overcome the defects from which it suffers.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Sales, 415-286-8495 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Ryan Stevens, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court asks questions. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security.

The Court adopts the tentative ruing. Grant in part. No timely opposition filed. On June 7, 2019, Mr. Sales filed a one-page "Opposition" that does not state any intelligible ground for opposing the motion.

The Court finds that Plaintiff James Sales, having within the past year commenced in propria persona six lawsuits against the City and County of San Francisco that have been finally determined adversely to him, is a vexatious litigant. (Code Civ. Proc. 391(b)(1).) Because the Court has sustained the City's demurrer to the amended complaint in this action without leave to amend, it denies the City's request that it require Plaintiff to furnish security under Code of Civil Procedure Section 391.1, a provision which only applies to "pending" litigation "at any time until final judgment is entered." However, on the Court's own motion, the Court will enter a prefiling order which prohibits Mr. Sales from filing any new litigation in the courts of this state in propria persona without first obtaining leave of the presiding judge of the court where the litigation is proposed to be filed. (Code Civ. Proc. 391.7(a); see Shalant v. Girardi (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1164, 1169-1171.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant'S Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRENT RHORER'S Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security

Defendant Trent Rhorer's motion to declare plaintiff a vexatious litigant is off calendar. Defendant served Plaintiff at 1539 Shafter Avenue, Unit B, San Francisco, CA 94134. Plaintiff's original complaint, amended complaint, and the court's docket indicate that Plaintiff resides at 5023 3rd Street, San Francisco, CA 94124.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
